arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta told decision on Arsenal star cost Gunners in Bournemouth defeat
arts.sum: Declan Rice gave Arsenal the lead in Saturday's clash with Bournemouth but he was off the pitch when the visitors completed their comeback late on and earned a 2-1 win

arts.ttl: How Bournemouth turned tables on set-piece specialists Arsenal
arts.sum: Match of the Day pundit Joe Hart shows how "streetwise" Bournemouth punished Arsenal from set-pieces.

arts.ttl: Arsenal ratings: Gunners poor beyond Rice, Ødegaard in loss to Bournemouth
arts.sum: Arsenal made it three league games without a win as a poor second half swung things in Bournemouth's favor. The one plus: no injuries ahead of a pivotal Champions League semifinal, second leg, at PSG this Wednesday.

arts.ttl: Arsenal to use 'anger and rage' of Bournemouth loss against PSG
arts.sum: Arsenal will use the "anger, rage and frustration" from their home loss to Bournemouth against PSG on Wednesday, says boss Mikel Arteta.

arts.ttl: Arsenal’s preparations for Paris St Germain clash dented by Bournemouth comeback
arts.sum: Second-half goals by Dean Huijsen and Evanilson helped Bournemouth complete a Premier League double over Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Premier League issue clarification on VAR drama in Arsenal vs Bournemouth
arts.sum: Arsenal hosted Bournemouth in the Premier League on Saturday and were 1-0 up before the visitors pulled two goals back - but their second goal was at the centre of VAR drama

arts.ttl: Arsenal v Bournemouth team news: Saka starts, Semenyo benched
arts.sum: Two changes for Arsenal, three for the Cherries The post Arsenal v Bournemouth team news: Saka starts, Semenyo benched appeared first on Best FPL Tips, Advice, Team News, Picks, and Statistics from Fantasy Football Scout.

arts.ttl: Arsenal v Bournemouth: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Premier League updates from the 5.30pm BST kick-off Live scoreboard | Latest tables | And you can email Scott Jurriën Timber is indeed injured, but his omission today sounds precautionary rather than something more serious. Here’s the latest, hot off the press, courtesy of our man at the Emirates, David Hytner. Jurrien Timber is nursing a minor knock – the one that forced him off in the first-leg of Arsenal’s Champions League semi-final against Paris Saint-Germain on Tuesday – and so Mikel Arteta has left him of the squad for today’s visit of Bournemouth. In comes Ben White. But the Arsenal manager has made only one other change: Thomas Partey in, Mikel Merino down to the bench. Partey missed out against PSG because of suspension and he was missed. Arteta is plainly in the mood to ignite some momentum ahead of Wednesday’s second-leg in Paris. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Dean Huijsen to Arsenal plunged into doubt as new £50m transfer theory emerges
arts.sum: Dean Huijsen to Arsenal makes "a lot of sense", Charles Watts believes, but he fears that the Gunners may not be as well-positioned as other clubs in the race.

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs. Bournemouth: Preview, Predictions and Lineups
arts.sum: Arsenal host Bournemouth in the Premier League on Saturday. Preview includes team news, how to watch on TV, score prediction and more.
